summary: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new product for generating designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers with Claude, extending the company from chat and coding into visual creation workflows. ByteDance Seed also surfaced multiple concrete model artifacts — Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM — while Baidu’s open-weight ERNIE-Image claimed state-of-the-art text-to-image performance with an 8B-parameter DiT, signaling continued competition in multimodal foundation models.

top_topics.HEADLINE: ByteDance Seed Debuts Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, giving an early public signal of the capabilities of its next-generation model line.
The company also introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, emphasizing attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking in voice interaction.
The announcements arrived alongside ByteDance Seed’s 2027 foundation model campus recruitment push, indicating continued investment in long-horizon model research and talent acquisition.

Coverage from Anthropic and TechCrunch indicates this is a standalone Labs product, not just a feature update inside the core Claude assistant.

top_topics.title: baidu/ERNIE-Image
top_topics.summary: Baidu released ERNIE-Image, an open text-to-image generation model. The model is built on a single-stream Diffusion Transformer architecture with 8B DiT parameters.

items.title: baidu/ERNIE-Image
items.summary: ERNIE-Image is an open text-to-image generation model developed by the ERNIE-Image team at Baidu. It is built on a single-stream Diffusion Transformer (DiT), with only 8B DiT parameters, it reaches state-of-the-art performance among open-weight text-to-image models.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Baidu released ERNIE-Image, an open text-to-image generation model. The model is built on a single-stream Diffusion Transformer architecture with 8B DiT parameters.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If Baidu’s performance claims hold, ERNIE-Image strengthens the open-weight image generation stack and raises the competitive floor for proprietary multimodal model providers.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Baidu Open-Sources ERNIE-Image, an 8B DiT Text-to-Image Model
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Baidu described ERNIE-Image as an open text-to-image model built on a single-stream Diffusion Transformer architecture.
The company said the model uses 8B DiT parameters while reaching state-of-the-art performance among open-weight text-to-image models.
An 8B-parameter open release from a major Chinese lab could make high-end image generation more accessible for downstream fine-tuning, regional deployment, and commercial integration.

items.title: Adding a new content type to my blog-to-newsletter tool
items.summary:
Agentic Engineering Patterns >

Here's an example of a deceptively short prompt that got a lot of work done in a single shot.

First, some background. I send out a free Substack newsletter around once a week containing content copied-and-pasted from my blog. I'm effectively using Substack as a lightweight way to allow people to subscribe to my blog via email.

I generate the newsletter with my blog-to-newsletter tool - an HTML and JavaScript app that fetches my latest content from this Datasette instance and forma...

topics.title: A Mechanistic Account of Attention Sinks in GPT-2: One Circuit, Broader Implications for Mitigation
topics.summary: Transformers commonly exhibit an attention sink: disproportionately high attention to the first position. We study this behavior in GPT-2-style models with learned query biases and absolute positional embeddings. Combining structural analysis with causal interventions, validated across natural-language, mathematical, and code inputs, we find that the sink arises from the interaction among (i) a learned query bias, (ii) the first-layer MLP transformation of the positional encoding, and (iii) structure in the key projection. Crucially, each component we identify is individually dispensable: a...

items.title: A Mechanistic Account of Attention Sinks in GPT-2: One Circuit, Broader Implications for Mitigation

Crucially, each component we identify is individually dispensable: a...

topics.title: Gating Enables Curvature: A Geometric Expressivity Gap in Attention
topics.summary: A research paper develops a geometric theory for attention mechanisms with multiplicative gating. It argues that gating expands attention from flat statistical manifolds to curved ones.

items.title: Gating Enables Curvature: A Geometric Expressivity Gap in Attention
items.summary: Broadly important today: Provides a geometric theory showing multiplicative gating expands attention from flat to curved statistical manifolds.

metadata.comment: Provides a geometric theory showing multiplicative gating expands attention from flat to curved statistical manifolds.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Provides a geometric theory showing multiplicative gating expands attention from flat to curved statistical manifolds.

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: A research paper develops a geometric theory for attention mechanisms with multiplicative gating. It argues that gating expands attention from flat statistical manifolds to curved ones.
topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: This research paper surfaced across 1 independent source(s).
topics.HEADLINE: Gating Enables Curvature: A Geometric Expressivity Gap in Attention

topics.title: On the Expressive Power and Limitations of Multi-Layer SSMs
topics.summary: A research paper analyzes the expressive power and limitations of multi-layer state space models. The summary also notes online chain-of-thought as a mechanism discussed alongside these expressivity results.

items.title: On the Expressive Power and Limitations of Multi-Layer SSMs
items.summary: The combination of rigorous SSM expressivity results with online CoT as a capability-transforming mechanism feels like a potentially new foundational direction.

metadata.comment: Formal expressivity limits for multi-layer SSMs and proof that online chain-of-thought lifts them to streaming-algorithm power.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The combination of rigorous SSM expressivity results with online CoT as a capability-transforming mechanism feels like a potentially new foundational direction.

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: A research paper analyzes the expressive power and limitations of multi-layer state space models. The summary also notes online chain-of-thought as a mechanism discussed alongside these expressivity results.

topics.title: This charming gadget writes bad AI poetry
topics.summary: The article reviews the Poetry Camera, a gadget that generates AI-written poetry based on what it captures. It describes the device’s design and the quality of its generated output.

items.title: This charming gadget writes bad AI poetry
items.summary: I've never been as charmed and frustrated by one gadget as I have with the Poetry Camera. It's a delightful object. White and cherry red with a color-matched woven strap, it looks playful and adorably lo-fi. If I saw it on a store shelf, I'd absolutely pick it up. But aside from obviously appealing, I'm […]

topics.title: From hours to minutes: How Agentic AI gave marketers time back for what matters
topics.summary: AWS published a case-study style post on how its marketing technology team worked with Gradial to build an agentic AI workflow on Amazon Bedrock. The system is described as accelerating content publishing for marketing operations.

items.title: From hours to minutes: How Agentic AI gave marketers time back for what matters
items.summary: In this post, we share how AWS Marketing’s Technology, AI, and Analytics (TAA) team worked with Gradial to build an agentic AI solution on Amazon Bedrock for accelerating content publishing workflows.

paper_spotlight.label: New Frontier Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that appear to open a genuinely new direction, paradigm, or field.

items.title: On the Expressive Power and Limitations of Multi-Layer SSMs
items.summary: The combination of rigorous SSM expressivity results with online CoT as a capability-transforming mechanism feels like a potentially new foundational direction.
items.spotlight_comment: The combination of rigorous SSM expressivity results with online CoT as a capability-transforming mechanism feels like a potentially new foundational direction.

items.title: Thermodynamic Diffusion Inference with Minimal Digital Conditioning
items.summary: This appears to open a genuinely new inference paradigm at the boundary of ML systems and physical computing.
items.spotlight_comment: This appears to open a genuinely new inference paradigm at the boundary of ML systems and physical computing.

items.title: Three-Phase Transformer
items.summary: The phase-structured residual-stream design is unconventional enough to look like a genuinely new architectural direction.
items.spotlight_comment: The phase-structured residual-stream design is unconventional enough to look like a genuinely new architectural direction.

items.title: Structure as Computation: Developmental Generation of Minimal Neural Circuits
items.summary: It proposes an unusual developmental-program view of building neural circuits, which feels like an early new direction rather than a routine model tweak.
items.spotlight_comment: It proposes an unusual developmental-program view of building neural circuits, which feels like an early new direction rather than a routine model tweak.

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: World-Value-Action Model: Implicit Planning for Vision-Language-Action Systems
items.summary: Embodied world-model planning in VLA systems is a broadly active area, so this likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed too.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: Embodied world-model planning in VLA systems is a broadly active area, so this likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed too.

items.title: When Flat Minima Fail: Characterizing INT4 Quantization Collapse After FP32 Convergence
items.summary: Broadly important today: Identifies a three-phase training dynamic where INT4 post-training quantization collapses after FP32 convergence despite stable perplexity.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Identifies a three-phase training dynamic where INT4 post-training quantization collapses after FP32 convergence despite stable perplexity.

items.title: Stability and Generalization in Looped Transformers
items.summary: Broadly important today: Fixed-point framework explains when looped transformers achieve stable, input-dependent extrapolation, highlighting recall and outer normalization.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Fixed-point framework explains when looped transformers achieve stable, input-dependent extrapolation, highlighting recall and outer normalization.

items.title: Gating Enables Curvature: A Geometric Expressivity Gap in Attention
items.summary: Broadly important today: Provides a geometric theory showing multiplicative gating expands attention from flat to curved statistical manifolds.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Provides a geometric theory showing multiplicative gating expands attention from flat to curved statistical manifolds.

items.title: Expressivity of Transformers: A Tropical Geometry Perspective
items.summary: Broadly important today: Derives transformer expressivity through tropical geometry, including exact partition characterizations and depth/head scaling laws.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Derives transformer expressivity through tropical geometry, including exact partition characterizations and depth/head scaling laws.

items.title: Blazing the trails before beating the path: Sample-efficient Monte-Carlo planning
items.summary: Broadly important today: Develops sample-efficient Monte Carlo planning with guarantees depending on near-optimal reachable states rather than full search space size.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Develops sample-efficient Monte Carlo planning with guarantees depending on near-optimal reachable states rather than full search space size.
